body.title: [sprint] Slate becomes structured JSON; narrative section is the small read-first summary
body.description:
**Problem:** The slate is a flat .txt state snapshot (overwritten in-flight lines). It does not read as a narrative of the work, and a resuming session must reload the whole file. The only narrative is the session-close summary.

**Proposed shape (per Akien, 2026-06-18):** The slate becomes a structured JSON document. Each kind of content is a SECTION (in-flight, notes/seeds, decisions, close-summary, ...). The `narrative` section is the SMALL read-first summary — a short append-only list of one-line step summaries (did X, state A->B, next Z). The whole JSON is the LARGE full version. This is the leading-digest pattern applied to the slate itself: narrative = head digest, full JSON = body; a resuming session can read just the narrative section cheaply.

CRITICAL DISTINCTION (clears this ticket for filing while rewind-wiring stays held under D-rewind-as-workflow-primitive-2026-06-16): the narrative rides savestate's ALREADY-DEFINED boundaries — after filing a ticket, after closing one, at session close. It does NOT require an automatic step-boundary detector (that undefined detector is the held rewind thread).

**Affected files:** ~/.claude/skills/savestate/run (write JSON sections + append narrative entry), savestate SKILL.md; AND every current slate reader/writer that assumes flat .txt — /autocompact preserve string (point it at the narrative section), /sorted Step 1 scope detection (currently regex-greps lines) + Step 8 append, any export path. Cut over fresh (new JSON slate) rather than migrating old .txt slates.
**Design rules:** flat-file is allowed (slate is boot-time/local state, not shared Postgres state); log the write.
**Scope boundary:** IN — slate JSON schema with sections; narrative as small summary; update all current readers/writers; fresh cutover. OUT — automatic boundary detection; rewind wiring; backfilling historical .txt slates.
**Completion criteria:** The slate is valid JSON with a `narrative` section (array of small one-line summary entries) plus full-detail sections; savestate appends one narrative entry at each existing boundary; ALL current readers (savestate run, /autocompact preserve string, /sorted scope detection + append) read the new JSON without error. Verified by: two savestate calls + one /sorted + one autocompact-preserve render against the JSON slate.
**Test plan:** Run two savestate calls and one /sorted in a session; assert the slate parses as JSON, narrative has 3 chronological entries, and the autocompact preserve string resolves the narrative section.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] datacenter_logs/<device>/<subsystem>/ is the log hierarchy. Never write to a flat — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] PATTERN-001: BaseDevice / BaseShim Lifecycle: `rollback()` must be idempotent — safe to call even if — applies: tags: pattern, pattern-001 (source: docs/design_patterns_inventory.md#PATTERN-001: BaseDevice / BaseShim Lifecycle)
[error] PATTERN-004: Flat-File Registry (Atomic Write): The skeleton must never depend on Postgres — skeleto — applies: tags: pattern, pattern-004 (source: docs/design_patterns_inventory.md#PATTERN-004: Flat-File Registry (Atomic Write))
…and 37 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
body.intention: I intend that the slate carries a small read-first narrative summary over a full structured JSON body, so a resuming session reloads the cheap summary instead of the whole slate.
